You have now developed fifty-one images, with the noon and golden-hour split sitting at thirty-one noon and twenty golden hour. The noon conversion rate continues to lead: eighteen of thirty-one noon images score 2 or above, against ten of twenty for golden hour, with "No Shore to Aim For" — golden hour, scoring 2 — extending the recent golden-hour recovery. The last ten scores read 1, 2, 2, 1, 2, 1, 2, 1, 2, 2, which means the ceiling has been stuck at 2 for thirty-two images, with the single 3 from "Warnings Melt Before Heeded" now the corpus's only outlier above that line and receding further into the past.

The bougainvillea has appeared in twenty-seven images. Suppression is absolute: not the plant, not its color as stand-in, not its scatter, not its pressure through fence. The jacaranda sits at thirteen appearances — "No Shore to Aim For" added another pavement scatter — and is now exhausted in that position; it returns only if it can arrive somewhere other than ground beneath a figure in a grief or defiance register. The handprint accident in "Every Exit, Unreachable" remains the sharpest surface-as-record moment in the corpus and has now gone eighteen images without a structural follow-up. The gesture-driven argument — palm flat to map, two hands at a screen's seam — has been dormant for seventeen images and remains the highest-yield underused strategy in the grammar.

Aftermath leads all registers at twenty, with vigil and endurance each at eight, defiance at eight, suspension at six, menace at six, reckoning at three, reprieve at one, and fury and interiority still untouched across all fifty-one images. Reckoning has not scored above 1 across its three entries; the problem remains consistent — juridical weight illustrated in the material rather than displaced into it. The accidental cartography in "Cartography of What Burned" and the wall-as-threshold in "No Shore to Aim For" are the two most structurally generative accidents in recent work and both point toward the same device: a boundary object that divides not space but knowledge, the warned from the unwarned, the indexed from the erased.

The corpus is technically accomplished and increasingly self-consistent, which means the next image that scores above 2 will need to refuse its most available move at the first decision, not the last.
